lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.title: NBA The Run Gameplay Overview
lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.intro: NBA The Run is an online 3v3 street basketball game built for speed, style, and knockout pressure. The core loop is simple: pick your players, enter Solos or Squads, play fast matches on iconic courts, win to advance, and earn Cred.

cards.name: NBA The Run Online 3v3 Street Basketball
cards.description: NBA The Run is built around fast-paced online 3v3 basketball on streetball courts around the world. Players take NBA stars and streetball legends into short, high-energy matchups where winning keeps the run alive.

cards.name: NBA The Run Arcade-Style Offense
cards.description: The offense is designed around posterizing dunks, stepback logo threes, ankle-breaking tricks, alley-oops, spin moves, and mid-air decisions like switching from a dunk to a layup to avoid a block.

cards.name: NBA The Run Defense Is a Core Feature
cards.description: Defense is treated as a major part of the fun, not just a support role. Blocks, steals, diving for loose balls, and physical defensive plays can create momentum swings and lead directly to wins.

cards.name: NBA The Run Rollback Netcode for Online Play
cards.description: NBA The Run uses true rollback netcode so actions like crossovers, loose-ball dives, and alley-oops stay synchronized for online players. This supports the game's focus on fast, responsive multiplayer control.

cards.name: NBA The Run Tournament-First Match Flow
cards.description: Instead of only presenting standalone matches, NBA The Run centers on Knockout Tournaments. Players enter, win, advance, and repeat across short rounds with randomized rulesets that change how each game feels.

highlights: NBA The Run Defense Momentum Swings
NBA The Run True Rollback Netcode
NBA The Run Knockout Tournament Flow

lucidBlocksWorldRegions.title: NBA The Run Beginner Guide
lucidBlocksWorldRegions.intro: NBA The Run beginner progress is fastest when you learn the knockout format first, then build your offense, defense, momentum timing, and practice routine in that order.

regions.name: NBA The Run Step 1: Choose Solos or Squads
regions.description: Start by choosing how much control you want. In Knockout Solos, you build and control your own team of three. In Knockout Squads, you control one player on a three-player squad. Beginner tip: Pick Solos for full team control or Squads for a co-op role experience.

regions.name: NBA The Run Step 2: Understand the Knockout Format
regions.description: Every run is based on a four-round knockout tournament. Win to advance and adapt to randomized rulesets each round. Beginner tip: Treat each round as a short challenge and learn the rule twist fast.

regions.name: NBA The Run Step 3: Learn the Offensive Flow
regions.description: Offense is fast and highlight-driven, with poster dunks, stepback threes, crossovers, alley-oops, spin moves, and quick rim reads. Beginner tip: Play with pace and timing instead of slowing every possession.

regions.name: NBA The Run Step 4: Do Not Ignore Defense
regions.description: Defense is as important as offense. Blocks, steals, physical pressure, and loose-ball dives create direct scoring chances. Beginner tip: Learn when to pressure, when to contest, and when to gamble for a steal.

regions.name: NBA The Run Step 5: Use In the Zone Momentum
regions.description: In the Zone is a momentum system that unlocks powerful signature abilities. Big plays charge it faster, and timing can flip close games. Beginner tip: Save your strongest momentum moment for key possessions.

regions.name: NBA The Run Step 6: Earn Cred and Unlock Style Rewards
regions.description: Winning matches earns Cred for cosmetics like jerseys, advanced dunks, taunts, badges, banners, and Rookie Variants. Beginner tip: Build a style you enjoy first, then scale your unlock plan.

regions.name: NBA The Run Step 7: Practice Before Competitive Runs
regions.description: Shootaround is the core practice space, while Knockout Friends supports private tournament setups versus AI, with friends, or against invited players. Beginner tip: Use practice and private play to lock in movement and finishing timing.

lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.title: NBA The Run Game Modes
lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.intro: NBA The Run launches with three confirmed knockout modes. Each one changes who you control, how team setup works, and how social or private play is handled.

creatures.name: NBA The Run Knockout Squads
creatures.description: You control one player on a squad of three and fill the team with friends or matched players. Best for role-based team play where each person controls one athlete.

creatures.name: NBA The Run Knockout Solos
creatures.description: You build and control your own team of three against other three-player teams. Best for players who want full control over switching, offense, defense, and match decisions.

creatures.name: NBA The Run Knockout Friends
creatures.description: Private tournament format with the widest setup range: play solo versus AI, team up with friends versus AI, or run invited head-to-head competition. Supports up to 48 players at launch.

lucidBlocksMobilityGear.title: NBA The Run Knockout Tournament
lucidBlocksMobilityGear.intro: NBA The Run tournaments run on a four-round knockout structure: enter, survive short 3v3 rounds, adapt to ruleset changes, reach the finals court, and run it back with Cred rewards.

items.name: NBA The Run Step 1: Enter a Knockout Tournament
items.description: Every game starts as a four-round knockout run. Entry can come through Solos, Squads, or private Knockout Friends setup. Player goal: Start the run and prepare to win multiple short rounds.

items.name: NBA The Run Step 2: Play Fast 3v3 Rounds
items.description: Teams of three play quick-burst rounds on iconic streetball courts around the world. Player goal: Win the current matchup and stay alive in the bracket.

items.name: NBA The Run Step 3: Adapt to Randomized Rulesets
items.description: Each round can include a randomized ruleset that changes offensive, defensive, and momentum decisions. Player goal: Read the rule twist quickly and adjust before opponents do.

items.name: NBA The Run Step 4: Win to Advance
items.description: The loop is direct: win, advance, repeat. A loss ends the run, while a win moves your team toward the final. Player goal: Stack wins across all four rounds.

items.name: NBA The Run Step 5: Reach the Championship Game
items.description: Each tournament ends on one of four designated finals courts where the Run the World trophy is decided. Player goal: Win the final and complete the run.

items.name: NBA The Run Step 6: Earn Cred and Run It Back
items.description: Winning rounds and tournaments earns Cred for cosmetics including jerseys, advanced dunks, taunts, badges, banners, and Rookie Variants. Player goal: Customize your style, then queue the next run.

unlockMilestones: NBA The Run Enter Through Solos, Squads, or Friends
NBA The Run Adapt to Randomized Rulesets
NBA The Run Win the Championship Game
NBA The Run Convert Wins Into Cred Unlocks

lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.title: NBA The Run Roster
lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.intro: NBA THE RUN centers its roster around high-impact streetball roles: shooters, dunkers, defensive anchors, playmakers, young two-way stars, and Street Legends. The launch pool includes more than 30 NBA stars, five Rookie Variants of NBA All-Stars, and five fictional Street Legends led by playable announcer Bobbito Garcia.

sections.name: NBA The Run Court Visionaries
sections.description: Type: NBA Stars. Players: LeBron James, Cade Cunningham, Tyrese Haliburton. Role focus: Primary playmaking, tempo control, passing, and half-court creation. Unlock context: Base roster reveal group.

sections.name: NBA The Run Green Light
sections.description: Type: NBA Stars. Players: Stephen Curry, Donovan Mitchell, Jalen Brunson. Role focus: Deep shooting, quick-release scoring, guard creation, and heat-check offense. Unlock context: Base roster reveal group.

sections.name: NBA The Run Midrange Masters
sections.description: Type: NBA Stars. Players: Kevin Durant, Devin Booker, Shai Gilgeous-Alexander. Role focus: Shot creation, midrange scoring, isolation offense, and clutch buckets. Unlock context: Base roster reveal group.

sections.name: NBA The Run Got Next
sections.description: Type: NBA Stars. Players: Cooper Flagg, Amen Thompson, Victor Wembanyama. Role focus: Young two-way upside, length, athleticism, and highlight plays. Unlock context: Base roster reveal group.

sections.name: NBA The Run Flight Club
sections.description: Type: NBA Stars. Players: Anthony Edwards, Ja Morant, Zion Williamson. Role focus: Vertical finishing, contact dunks, speed, and above-the-rim pressure. Unlock context: Base roster reveal group.

sections.name: NBA The Run Beasts in the East
sections.description: Type: NBA Stars. Players: Giannis Antetokounmpo, Jayson Tatum, Paolo Banchero. Role focus: Big-wing scoring, physical drives, defensive versatility, and takeover potential. Unlock context: Base roster reveal group.

sections.name: NBA The Run No Fly Zone
sections.description: Type: NBA Stars. Players: Bam Adebayo, Scottie Barnes, Anthony Davis. Role focus: Defense, rim protection, switching, steals, blocks, and loose-ball pressure. Unlock context: Base roster reveal group.

sections.name: NBA The Run Triple Threat MVPs
sections.description: Type: NBA Stars. Players: Nikola Jokić, James Harden, Joel Embiid. Role focus: MVP-level scoring, passing, post pressure, and matchup control. Unlock context: Base roster reveal group.

sections.name: NBA The Run Special Delivery
sections.description: Type: NBA Stars. Players: LaMelo Ball, Trae Young, Tyrese Maxey. Role focus: Fast guards, creative passing, pace, pull-up shooting, and quick offense. Unlock context: Base roster reveal group.

sections.name: NBA The Run Stat Sheet Stuffers
sections.description: Type: NBA Stars. Players: Luka Dončić, Josh Giddey, Jaylen Brown. Role focus: All-around creation, rebounding help, scoring balance, and flexible team fit. Unlock context: Base roster reveal group.

sections.name: NBA The Run Community Additions
sections.description: Type: NBA Stars. Players: Kawhi Leonard, Damian Lillard. Role focus: Two-way scoring, elite shot-making, perimeter pressure, and late-game offense. Unlock context: Added to the launch roster through community response.

sections.name: NBA The Run Rookie Variants
sections.description: Type: Alternate NBA Star Versions. Players: LeBron James '03, Kevin Durant '07, Stephen Curry '09, Luka Dončić '18, Shai Gilgeous-Alexander '18. Role focus: Alternate early-career versions with different looks and play styles. Unlock context: Available through The Shop and progression rewards.

sections.name: NBA The Run Street Legends
sections.description: Type: Fictional Streetball Legends. Players: Bobbito Garcia and four additional fictional Street Legends. Role focus: Stylized streetball builds with major strengths, clear weaknesses, and personality-driven play. Unlock context: Unlocked by ranking up.

lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.title: NBA The Run Best Players Watchlist
lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.intro: NBA THE RUN is built around short online Knockout rounds, fast momentum swings, In the Zone abilities, and streetball-style team building. The best picks are players who can create instant offense, protect the rim, punish defenders in space, or fit cleanly beside two other stars.

priorities.name: NBA The Run 3v3 Takeover Cores
priorities.description: Players: LeBron James, Luka Dončić, Nikola Jokić, Giannis Antetokounmpo, Victor Wembanyama. Best for: Building a squad around one dominant creator or matchup breaker. Team fit: Pair with one shooter and one defensive finisher for balanced Solos or Squads play.

priorities.name: NBA The Run Elite Shot Creators
priorities.description: Players: Stephen Curry, Kevin Durant, Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, Devin Booker, Jayson Tatum, Damian Lillard. Best for: Spacing the court, creating late-clock buckets, and punishing overhelp. Team fit: Pair with a rim runner or defensive anchor so scoring gravity opens easy finishes.

priorities.name: NBA The Run Rim Pressure Finishers
priorities.description: Players: Anthony Edwards, Ja Morant, Zion Williamson, Anthony Davis, Joel Embiid, Paolo Banchero. Best for: Dunks, contact finishes, paint pressure, and quick momentum swings. Team fit: Pair with a passer or shooter to keep driving lanes open.

priorities.name: NBA The Run Defense and Two-Way Control
priorities.description: Players: Bam Adebayo, Scottie Barnes, Kawhi Leonard, Jaylen Brown, Amen Thompson, Cooper Flagg. Best for: Stopping drives, switching matchups, chasing loose balls, and turning defense into offense. Team fit: Pair with a primary scorer so defensive stops quickly become transition points.

priorities.name: NBA The Run Speed Guards and Passers
priorities.description: Players: LaMelo Ball, Trae Young, Tyrese Maxey, Donovan Mitchell, Jalen Brunson, Tyrese Haliburton. Best for: Fast possessions, pull-up offense, ankle-breaking moves, and quick passing reads. Team fit: Pair with a dunker and a defender to cover size while keeping the offense explosive.

priorities.name: NBA The Run Flexible Team Builders
priorities.description: Players: Cade Cunningham, Josh Giddey, James Harden, Tyrese Haliburton, Kevin Durant '07, Stephen Curry '09. Best for: Creative squad experiments, alternate play styles, and role-based lineup building. Team fit: Use these picks to fill the missing skill in a lineup: passing, shooting, size, or pace.

lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.title: NBA The Run In The Zone Abilities
lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.intro: In NBA THE RUN, signature abilities work like a streetball takeover moment: make big plays, fill momentum, activate at the right time, and force the other team to adjust. The system rewards timing, defense, rim pressure, and star-specific play styles.

achievements.title: NBA The Run Posterizer Best Use
achievements.description: Role: Rim Pressure. Player example: Giannis Antetokounmpo. Attack the basket, finish through contact, and turn a dunk attempt into a momentum swing.

achievements.title: NBA The Run Posterizer Squad Tip
achievements.description: Use Posterizer with a spacing guard so defenders cannot collapse early without giving up a shot.

achievements.title: NBA The Run The Shadow Best Use
achievements.description: Role: Lockdown Defense. Player example: Jayson Tatum. Clamp ballhandlers, pressure late-clock possessions, and slow down isolation scorers.

achievements.title: NBA The Run The Shadow Squad Tip
achievements.description: Activate it when the opponent is trying to ride one scorer through a close round.

achievements.title: NBA The Run Ankle Breaker Best Use
achievements.description: Role: Handle Creation. Player example: Jalen Brunson. Create separation with crossovers, punish aggressive defenders, and open pull-up or drive windows.

achievements.title: NBA The Run Ankle Breaker Squad Tip
achievements.description: Pair an Ankle Breaker guard with a dunker waiting for help defense to rotate.

achievements.title: NBA The Run Spin Cycle Best Use
achievements.description: Role: Street Legend Handles. Player example: Bobbito Garcia. Use spin rhythm, handle pressure, and creative streetball movement to create space or passing angles.

achievements.title: NBA The Run Spin Cycle Squad Tip
achievements.description: Use it when defenders are overplaying the first move and chasing the ball too tightly.

achievements.title: NBA The Run Momentum Builder Best Use
achievements.description: Role: System Core. Player example: All players. Make big offensive or defensive plays to unlock In the Zone faster during short Knockout rounds.

achievements.title: NBA The Run Momentum Builder Squad Tip
achievements.description: Blocks, steals, alley-oops, poster dunks, and clean shot creation all help swing possession energy.

achievements.title: NBA The Run Takeover Timing Best Use
achievements.description: Role: Activation Strategy. Player example: All players. Save activation for the possession that decides a comeback, closes a round, or breaks a tied game.

achievements.title: NBA The Run Takeover Timing Squad Tip
achievements.description: Avoid spending a boost on low-leverage possessions and hold it for the swing possession.

lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.title: NBA The Run Controls and Skill Moves
lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.intro: NBA THE RUN is designed around responsive stick movement, rollback netcode, above-the-rim offense, and physical defense. Use this flow to practice what matters first before jumping into Solos, Squads, Shootaround, or Knockout Friends.

faqs.question: How should NBA The Run players move into space before each play?
faqs.answer: Start each possession by reading the defender, spacing the floor, and choosing whether to attack, pass, or reset. Use this before every drive or shot attempt so your first move has a clear purpose.

faqs.question: How do NBA The Run crossovers and ankle-breaking moves create advantages?
faqs.answer: Use quick direction changes to cross up aggressive defenders and create separation. Apply this against defenders overplaying one side, then chain one clean move into a shot, drive, or pass.

faqs.question: When should NBA The Run players attack with dunks and rim finishes?
faqs.answer: Attack open lanes with high-flying dunks and strong rim pressure when help defenders are late. Time your takeoff so defenders must choose between contesting the shot or giving up a pass.

faqs.question: How can NBA The Run players switch to a layup during a dunk attempt?
faqs.answer: Use a mid-air finish change when a shot blocker meets you at the rim or jumps early. Read the defender before takeoff, then switch only when the block is coming.

faqs.question: How does NBA The Run spin movement connect to alley-oop setups?
faqs.answer: Use a spin move to shift the defense, then throw a lob when a teammate cuts behind coverage or the rim protector steps toward the ball. Practice the timing between the spin, lane opening, and receiver jump.

faqs.question: What is the NBA The Run defensive priority for blocks, steals, and loose-ball dives?
faqs.answer: Use blocks at the rim, steals against predictable dribbles, and dives when the ball comes free after contact. Stay in position first, then punish bad timing instead of chasing every steal.

faqs.question: When should NBA The Run players activate In the Zone during a round?
faqs.answer: Build momentum through big plays, then activate a signature boost when one possession can decide the round, stop a comeback, or create a scoring run.

faqs.question: Where should NBA The Run players practice controls before competitive queues?
faqs.answer: Use Shootaround and Knockout Friends to repeat core sequences: one crossover into a drive, one mid-air finish change, one defensive stop, and one In the Zone activation.

lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.title: NBA The Run Progression
lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.intro: NBA THE RUN progression loops through wins, Cred, The Shop, ranking rewards, and trophy runs so players can prioritize the unlock path they care about most.

items.title: NBA The Run Cred

items.title: NBA The Run The Shop

items.title: NBA The Run Style Cosmetics

items.title: NBA The Run Rookie Variants

items.title: NBA The Run Tournament Trophies

questions.question: When does NBA The Run release?
questions.answer: NBA The Run is currently announced for a June 2026 launch window.

questions.question: How can I join the NBA The Run playtest?

questions.question: Where can I play NBA The Run?
questions.answer: NBA The Run is announced for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and Steam on PC.

questions.question: What modes are confirmed for NBA The Run?
questions.answer: NBA The Run showcases knockout-focused formats including solo, squad, and friend-based options.

questions.question: What are NBA The Run In The Zone abilities?
questions.answer: NBA The Run In The Zone abilities are momentum-driven boosts that can swing key possessions.

questions.question: How does NBA The Run progression work?
questions.answer: NBA The Run progression uses match performance and Cred to unlock cosmetics and identity options.
